methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $17M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 6 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Robotics & Manufacturing. Range: $300M - $10.0B.

data.narrative:
Hertha Metals is a Series A robotics and manufacturing company that has raised $17M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comparison universe of 9 private peers and 1 public comparable, all operating in the robotics and manufacturing sector at Series A or Series B stages. The peer set includes 1X Technologies ($10.0B), Bedrock Robotics ($1.8B), Dexterity ($1.6B), Collaborative Robotics ($500M), and Fairmat ($350M), among others.

Two triangulation methods are applicable. The stage-progression method applies a 2.0x step-up multiplier to the $17M in total capital raised, implying a valuation of $34M. The peer-median method benchmarks against six same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables with a median valuation of $1.1B, though the peer cohort exhibits substantial dispersion, ranging from $300M to $10.0B.

These two approaches produce a wide analytical range: a low bound of $29M (near the stage-progression output), a median of $554M, and a high bound of $1.2B anchored toward the peer median. The wide dispersion between stage-progression and peer-median results reflects the heterogeneity within the robotics sector at early stages—stage-progression is more conservative given the company's Series A position, while peer median is pulled upward by significantly more mature, Series B-stage capital raises. Key uncertainties include differentiation relative to the peer set, unit economics visibility, and path to Series B defensibility.
